name: Helm of Awe
description: The Helm of Awe (Ægishjálmr) is a magical Icelandic stave, not a literal helmet, that is a symbol of protection and invincibility. It is believed to grant the wearer power and dominance over their enemies, inducing fear and overwhelming them with an aura of authority. The symbol's design typically features eight arms radiating outwards from a central point, often with runes interspersed. Some interpretations connect these arms to the rune Algiz, representing protection, while others see connections to other protective runes. The Helm of Awe is not prominently featured in the Eddas or Sagas, its meaning and significance primarily derived from Icelandic grimoires and folklore. It represents not only physical protection but also psychological dominance, instilling fear and awe in opponents. Its power is tied to the wearer's mental strength and resolve, amplifying their inner power and projecting it outwards. The Helm of Awe is more than just a symbol; it is a conduit for channeling willpower and courage, a potent tool for overcoming adversity and asserting one's will. It is a reminder of the power of inner strength and the ability to inspire fear and respect through sheer force of will. While its exact origins remain shrouded in mystery, the Helm of Awe continues to be a powerful symbol in modern neo-paganism and Norse-inspired traditions.
subtitle: Symbol of Protection and Dominance

keyMyths.title: Protection in Battle
keyMyths.description: The Helm of Awe was believed to grant warriors invincibility and instill fear in their enemies on the battlefield. It was a symbol of courage and strength. The power was in the mind of the user.

keyMyths.title: Domination and Control
keyMyths.description: Wearing the Helm of Awe was thought to give the wearer control over others, allowing them to dominate their will and influence their actions. It projects the inner will outwards. The symbol is a powerful one.

sources.author: Stephen Flowers
sources.work: Icelandic Magic: Practical Secrets of the Northern Grimoires
sources.description: Discusses the use of the Helm of Awe in Icelandic magical practices.
sources.text: Discusses the use of the Helm of Awe in Icelandic magical practices.

sources.author: Edred Thorsson
sources.work: Futhark: A Handbook of Rune Magic
sources.description: Explores the runic connections and symbolism associated with the Helm of Awe.
